things.name: Old Market Square (Stary Rynek)
things.description: One of Poland's finest Renaissance squares with colourful merchants' arcades, the 16th-century Town Hall and the famous mechanical goats that butt heads at noon

things.name: Poznan Town Hall
things.description: Magnificent Renaissance town hall rebuilt by Giovanni Battista di Quadro after 1550; the ground floor loggia and ceremonial hall are among Poland's finest Renaissance interiors

things.name: Poznan Cathedral (Archcathedral Basilica)
things.description: Poland's oldest cathedral on Cathedral Island (Ostrow Tumski); built in 968, it holds the tombs of the first Polish kings Mieszko I and Boleslaw the Brave

things.name: Museum of the First Piasts at Lednica
things.description: Open-air archaeological park at Lednica Lake with the 10th-century palace complex of Poland's founding dynasty; a UNESCO-listed cradle of the Polish state

things.name: National Museum Poznan
things.description: Major art museum with an outstanding collection of Polish art, medieval Silesian sculpture and European paintings from the 15th to 20th centuries

things.name: Poznan International Fair
things.description: One of Central Europe's oldest trade fair venues since 1921; the striking modernist exhibition halls host major international trade events throughout the year

things.name: Croissant Museum (Rogelmuseum)
things.description: Quirky museum dedicated to the St. Martin croissant, Poznan's most famous pastry; bake your own rogal marcinski and learn the pastry's 200-year history

things.name: Imperial Castle
things.description: Neo-Romanesque palace built by Kaiser Wilhelm II in 1910; now a cultural centre, it remains one of Poznan's most imposing buildings from the Prussian era

things.name: Malta Lake
things.description: Artificial reservoir east of the city centre with a rowing course, water sports facilities, a summer toboggan run and Poznan's major outdoor recreation area

things.name: Enea Stadium
things.description: Home of Lech Poznan with 42,837 capacity; built for UEFA Euro 2012 and one of Poland's finest grounds; the club has played in the Europa League group stage
